extra.message: Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into aiohttp.web.Response, where it is used to render an HTML page returned to the user. This may result in a Cross-Site Scripting attack (XSS).
Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into django.http.HttpResponse, where it is used to render an HTML page returned to the user. This may result in a Cross-Site Scripting attack (XSS).
Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into django.http.HttpResponse, where it is used to render an HTML page returned to the user. This may result in a Cross-Site Scripting attack (XSS).

Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into fastapi.responses.HTMLResponse, where it is used to render an HTML page returned to the user. This may result in a Cross-Site Scripting attack (XSS).
Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into os.system, where it is used as a shell command. This may result in a Command Injection vulnerability.

xml.dom.pulldom.parseString is vulnerable to XXE and DDOS in versions of python <= 3.10. If using python runtime 3.10 or older, consider upgrading python versions, or see https://docs.python.org/3.10/library/xml.html#xml-vulnerabilities for mitigations. Python versions 3.11 and onwards do not have this issue.

xml.sax.parseString is vulnerable to XXE and DDOS in versions of python <= 3.10. If using python runtime 3.10 or older, consider upgrading python versions, or see https://docs.python.org/3.10/library/xml.html#xml-vulnerabilities for mitigations. Python versions 3.11 and onwards do not have this issue.

xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring is vulnerable to XXE and DDOS in versions of python <= 3.10. If using python runtime 3.10 or older, consider upgrading python versions, or see https://docs.python.org/3.10/library/xml.html#xml-vulnerabilities for mitigations. Python versions 3.11 and onwards do not have this issue.
jinja2.Environment is called with no autoescape argument (autoescaping is disabled by default). This increases the risk of Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks.
hashlib.md5 is insecure. Consider changing it to a secure hashing algorithm.
hashlib.md5 is insecure. Consider changing it to a secure hashing algorithm.
